research_question: After round7 showed that new orthogonal keepers still become mixed once promoted into the frozen v18 core family, which additional atomic price-volume mechanisms focused on overnight behavior, gap handling, recovery quality, signed turnover asymmetry, and event-state persistence can expand the keeper pool without colliding with the current canonical alias map or simply remixing existing momentum, liquidity-trend, amount-shock, and confirmation clusters?

discovery_contract.builder_rule: each candidate builder may rank exactly one raw signal field and may not combine multiple raw signals inside the same candidate
discovery_contract.source_inspiration_rule: candidate definitions may draw inspiration from Alpha158-style atomic expressions and common price-volume paper mechanisms, but every field must be implemented independently inside this project and must not rely on the neighboring qlib adapter stack

signal_pool.interpretation: a higher share of positive overnight contribution may capture an overnight-strength mechanism distinct from intraday trend bias and close-to-close momentum

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This isolates close-to-next-open strength rather than open-to-close drift, so it tests a separate overnight channel.

signal_pool.interpretation: more consistent agreement between overnight direction and same-day intraday direction may capture a session-continuity mechanism distinct from plain price-volume correlation

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This focuses on cross-session directional continuity rather than contemporaneous return-amount correlation.

signal_pool.interpretation: stronger filling of downside gaps may capture recovery quality after overnight weakness, distinct from generic downside absorption

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This measures the fill ratio of downside gaps specifically, not average recovery strength across all weak sessions.

signal_pool.interpretation: stronger asymmetry in intraday recovery versus intraday fade may capture session-level resilience distinct from intraday trend bias

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests reversal asymmetry inside the session rather than average directional drift across sessions.

signal_pool.interpretation: more turnover concentrated on positive-return days may capture participation imbalance distinct from signed amount imbalance

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This replaces traded amount with turnover intensity and may capture a different participation-quality mechanism.

signal_pool.interpretation: higher entropy of daily traded amount may capture diversified activity persistence rather than one-sided spike behavior

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This measures dispersion regularity of amount through time, not short-vs-medium displacement in amount level.

signal_pool.interpretation: stronger followthrough after wide-range days may capture event-state continuation distinct from simple breakout proximity

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests what happens after large range events rather than static distance to a recent boundary.

signal_pool.interpretation: faster recovery after low-break events may capture structural repair capacity distinct from downside gap or downside absorption measures

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This is event-conditioned on low-break sessions rather than a broad weak-day absorption average.

signal_pool.interpretation: better ability to hold gains after opening high may capture gap-hold quality distinct from intraday trend bias

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This isolates hold quality after a strong open rather than broad directional range participation on up days.

signal_pool.interpretation: more stable overnight gap behavior may capture overnight fragility or order-flow balance distinct from downside gap frequency

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This focuses on stability of gap behavior across both directions rather than just counting downside gaps.

discovery_contract.aggregation_rule: none; this round explicitly forbids combining signals
discovery_contract.missing_data_rule: score nulls are allowed and must be preserved for audit; no imputation or cross-signal fallback is allowed

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard, same TopK=10 width, and same v18 top-15 refresh hysteresis
constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: same v18 refreshed-portfolio implementation until any later family round explicitly changes it

promotion_rule.phase_gate: Only atomic signals that show directionally coherent positive edge in signal-edge diagnostics may advance into a later composability-screening round.
promotion_rule.non_promotion_rule: Alias-like variants, inverted duplicates, or signals whose top slice remains weaker than rank11_20 should be rejected rather than rescued by later overlay or family tweaks.

disallowed_changes: Do not mix multiple raw signals into one candidate inside this round.
Do not change the calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard threshold or guard stage.
Do not change the frozen v18 refresh hysteresis rule.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not add extraction, weight-penalty, turnover-suppression, stability-cap, overlay, or family-composition changes inside this round.
Do not import or depend on the neighboring qlib data adapter layer; this project must implement all candidate fields independently on its own data contracts.
Do not open budget for candidates that are already covered by the canonical alias map unless a new override_reason is explicitly approved.
notes: This round reopens atomic discovery only after both intraday_trend_bias_20d_raw and upside_range_share_20d_raw passed standalone diagnostics but returned mixed composability screens against the frozen v18 core family.
The objective is to push into more orthogonal overnight, gap-handling, event-state, and resilience mechanisms rather than reworking existing trend-confirmation and amount-shock clusters.
